title: Wet-weather exemption for dry-compound debt
summary: Clear dry-compound obligation pressure once wet or intermediate running has appeared in the current driver-race sequence.
rationale: Weather-aware rule-state variants appeared technically plausible and avoided treating wet races as normal dry compound-rotation decisions. Pirelli describes intermediates and wets as distinct wet-weather tyre classes.
implementation_hint: Within the chronological driver-race slick-history block, maintain a current-or-prior wet-weather-seen flag for INTERMEDIATE or WET compounds. Set remaining dry-compound debt to zero or add an exempted flag when wet-weather running has occurred.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence; most useful if the dataset contains mixed-weather races.
risk: If wet-weather rows are rare, this may add sparse features with little effect; keep the block compact.
